name: Spaghetti Squash and Zucchini Casserole
recipeIngredient: or 1 6 oz can tomato paste + 2 oz water
1 tsp dried or finely cut fresh basil
1 tsp garlic powder or two cloves finely chopped fresh garlic
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut spaghetti squash in half length-wise.
2. Carefully scrape out seeds, but do not cut into the body of the squash.
3. While the squash is cooking, prepare other ingredients.
4. Stovetop: Place half squash cut side down in a pot with 2 inches (5 cm) water, cover and boil for 20 minutes.
5. Microwave: Place half squash in a dish with 1/4 cup water, cover with plastic wrap, and cook at high (>600 watts) for 7 minutes.
6. Scrape out squash to produce spaghetti-like strands. A thin, hard shell will remain.
7. In a large bowl, add spaghetti squash, tomato sauce, grated zucchini, and spices. Mix well.
8. Spoon mixture into squash shell (or casserole dish), top with nutritional yeast, a little more garlic powder, and optional non-fat grated parmesan cheese.
9. Bake at 350 degrees F. (175 C.) for 20 minutes or until done.
Nutritional Analysis:
Each 8 oz (.25 l) serving has 66 calories and less than 0.25 g fat.
Notes:
Served with brown rice and fresh non-fat French Bread. Ideal filling for a pita pocket or tortilla.
Other half of the squash can be refrigerated for a few days, then prepared as shown above, except microwave for 10 minutes or boil for 30 minutes, and scrape it out to serve topped with favorite spaghetti sauce.
Time:
This took about 8 minutes of working time, 7 minutes of microwaving while getting the rest ready, and waiting 20 minutes while baking. The smell and taste were delicious.
Makes: 6 servings.
